message:
fix(codegen): Convert non-string %s arguments to string with pyRepr

Python's %s format specifier calls str() on any argument type.
Zig's {s} format specifier requires a string type.

When using %s with a non-string argument (tuple, class instance, etc.),
wrap the argument in runtime.builtins.pyRepr() to convert to string.

This fixes format string type mismatch errors like:
  "Failed to raise TypeError with %s" % ((base, result),)
where a tuple was being passed directly to {s} format.
